creator: florian.heigl@gmail.com
text:
www.vuxml.org is the download site for the freebsd security information, delivered as a bz2 packed xml file.

as of recent, the CN for the certificate on the server is invalid, showing a wildcard certificate for *.freebsd.org, while the hostname pkg uses is 'www.vuxml.org'

it's important to be able to download this file via a secure means to detect tampering, especially since it's likely to be parsed by a root process reading in bz2 and xml, which both have their own history of exploits.


host:/# pkg audit -F              
SSL certificate subject doesn't match host www.vuxml.org
SSL certificate subject doesn't match host www.vuxml.org
SSL certificate subject doesn't match host www.vuxml.org
pkg: https://www.vuxml.org/freebsd/vuln.xml.bz2: Authentication error
pkg: cannot fetch vulnxml file

(this error will only show if you use https to fetch the list. If you don't you don't get the error, but it's also not recommendable to not use https for this!!!!!)


host:/# openssl s_client -connect www.vuxml.org:443 -showcerts      
CONNECTED(00000003)
depth=2 /C=US/ST=New Jersey/L=Jersey City/O=The USERTRUST Network/CN=USERTrust RSA Certification Authority
verify error:num=20:unable to get local issuer certificate
verify return:0
---
Certificate chain
 0 s:/OU=Domain Control Validated/OU=Gandi Standard Wildcard SSL/CN=*.freebsd.org
   i:/C=FR/ST=Paris/L=Paris/O=Gandi/CN=Gandi Standard SSL CA 2
-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----

...


This is a major messup and I hope someone can be contacted and fix this.
